notes: SHIP v1.14.287 - FIX-2 (B-01 / H2): bottom nav dead from Crash Cart. Root cause (owner-confirmed H2 via 'top-of-scroll revives the nav'): the #pg-ref scroller (.page L1055-1069, position:absolute;inset:0;overflow-y:auto;-webkit-overflow-scrolling:touch) composites its scrolled content OVER the fixed #rd-botnav (z-index:40, a sibling outside the scroller) on iOS Safari - the classic fixed-below-scroller compositing bug. Scrolled down the nav painted under content (dead + faint ghosting); scrolled to top the compositing settled and the nav returned. FIX (single property, redesign-only): transform:translateZ(0) on #rd-botnav (L9215) promotes it to its own GPU compositing layer so it always stacks above the scroller. Fixes all three pages, not just Crash Cart. Safe re the fixed-child trap: the nav's only positioned child (#bn-core, absolute) already anchors to the nav (a containing block as position:fixed); a transform on the nav itself keeps it viewport-fixed and just gives it a layer. #rd-botnav renders only under body.rd (legacy .tab-nav untouched) -> ?legacy=1 byte-identical. iOS-Safari-ONLY bug - desktop Chrome does not reproduce it, so this is DEVICE-VERIFY-ONLY. Three-stamp .286->.287. DEVICE-VERIFY (John, iPhone): Crash Cart -> deep-scroll the list -> tap COMMAND / WORK mid-scroll -> nav engages (was dead); no page content ghosting through the nav band at any scroll position; repeat on Work + Command. If translateZ(0) alone does not hold, escalation is will-change:transform / a parent isolation:isolate.
